_comment: Weekly fishing report — EDITOR FILE. Update these fields and redeploy: (1) `updated` = YYYY-MM-DD of this report; (2) `summary` = a few honest sentences; (3) each species `status` (one of: hot | good | fair | slow | unknown) + `note`. Rendered at the top of fishing.html (#report). HONESTY: if `updated` is more than 14 days old, the page automatically shows 'report from <date>' instead of implying it's current — so it's fine to leave it between updates. No invented catch numbers; keep it to what's actually known.
author: GoVallecito
summary: Season outlook (early summer): kokanee trolling typically ramps up as June warms — start at first light before the afternoon wind. Pike are active in the north-end shallows after ice-out, and stocked rainbows are reliable. This is a baseline outlook, not a live catch report — for this week's actual bite, ask at the marina or book a local guide. We'll post real updates here through the season.

species.name: Kokanee
species.note: Trolling builds as the water warms into summer; numbers vary year to year — ask the marina/guides for the current outlook.

species.name: Northern Pike
species.note: Active in the weedy north-end shallows in late spring and early fall.

species.name: Rainbow Trout
species.note: Reliable around ice-out and through spring, from boat or shore.

species.name: Walleye
species.note: Low-light bite; work 10–40 ft from a boat.

species.name: Smallmouth Bass
species.note: Rocky structure and docks as the shallows warm into June–July.
